segments.title: Type 26 orders and procurement scale
segments.summary: A Type 26 and Type 31 update frames Norway's order, Australian reductions, and British yard capacity as a procurement-scale problem.
segments.body: The discussion captures the episode's organizing procurement update. Dr. Clarke explains that the video is about changed strategy and procurement rather than unchanged ship statistics, then uses Australia's reduced Hunter order, Canada's plan, Norway's five Type 26s, and British production capacity to show how commonality and longer production runs can lower risk and cost.

evidence.note: The opening defines the update as a discussion of changed strategy, procurement, outside interest, and Type 31 additions.
The program-status section links British, Canadian, Australian, and Norwegian Type 26 decisions to total hull numbers, commonality, and cheaper high-end frigate procurement.
The Norway section discusses production-line pressure, possible British hull transfers, yard-capacity options, and continuity into the Type 83 program.
